content.text:
Please review the VenueReserve Operations Suite frontend and address the following gaps to achieve a full "Pass" on static audit:

Implement all prompt-required flows and constraints:

Advanced reservation rules (e.g., booking window, min/max duration, slot stitching, holiday/exception handling, check-in/no-show logic).
Full coupon logic (e.g., $/%, min/max, per-item, cart-wide, and real-time recalculation).
Audit trail and reconciliation UI (variance flags, shift/daily reports, audit trail browsing).
Moderation throttles (post/report rate limits, IP/device limits, CAPTCHA, allowlist/blocklist, ban durations).
Security/audit controls (UI for anomaly alerts, tamper-evident logs, security event browsing).
Expand state and error handling:

Ensure all user actions and edge cases have clear, user-facing loading, error, disabled, and success states.
Add duplicate submit protection and edge-case validation.
Add or expand tests:

Cover all roles, dashboards, and flows.
Test advanced reservation/coupon logic, audit/reconciliation, moderation/reporting, and all error/edge cases.
Refactor duplicated business logic into shared utilities where possible.

Document any remaining scope exclusions or limitations.

164: export function installRouteGuards(router) {
165:   router.beforeEach((to) => {
166:     const token = localStorage.getItem(AUTH_TOKEN_KEY);
167:     if (to.path !== "/login" && !token) {
168:       return "/login";
169:     }
170:     if (to.path === "/login" && token) {
171:       return "/";
172:     }
173: 
174:     const permission = to.meta?.permission;
175:     if (!permission) return true;
176:     const role = getActiveRole();
177:     if (hasPermission(role, permission)) return true;
178:     return "/access-denied";
179:   });
180: }

<content>1: import axios from "axios";
2: 
3: const STORAGE_PREFIX = "studio-cache";
4: const QUEUE_KEY = "studio-offline-queue";
5: export const ACTOR_ID_KEY = "studio-actor-user-id";
6: export const AUTH_TOKEN_KEY = "studio-auth-token";
7: 
8: const api = axios.create({
9:   baseURL: import.meta.env.VITE_API_BASE_URL || "http://localhost:4000/api/v1",
10:   timeout: 5000,
11: });
12: 
13: api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
14:   const headers = { ...(config.headers || {}) };
15:   const token = localStorage.getItem(AUTH_TOKEN_KEY);
16:   if (token) {
17:     headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
18:   }
19:   return { ...config, headers };
20: });
21: 
22: export function getStoredAuthToken() {
23:   return localStorage.getItem(AUTH_TOKEN_KEY) || null;
24: }
25: 
26: export function clearStoredAuthToken() {
27:   localStorage.removeItem(AUTH_TOKEN_KEY);
28: }
29: 
30: export function clearStoredActorUserId() {
31:   localStorage.removeItem(ACTOR_ID_KEY);
32: }
33: 
34: export async function loginWithPassword({ email, password }) {
35:   const response = await api.post("/auth/login", { email, password });
36:   const data = response.data;
37:   if (data?.token) {
38:     localStorage.setItem(AUTH_TOKEN_KEY, data.token);
39:   }
40:   if (data?.user_id) {
41:     localStorage.setItem(ACTOR_ID_KEY, data.user_id);
42:   }
43:   return data;
44: }
45: 
46: function cacheKey(entity) {
47:   return `${STORAGE_PREFIX}:${entity}`;
48: }
49: 
50: function readJSON(key, fallback = null) {
51:   const value = localStorage.getItem(key);
52:   if (!value) return fallback;
53:   try {
54:     return JSON.parse(value);
55:   } catch {
56:     return fallback;
57:   }
58: }
59: 
60: function writeJSON(key, value) {
61:   localStorage.setItem(key, JSON.stringify(value));
62: }
63: 
64: export function getCachedList(entity) {
65:   return readJSON(cacheKey(entity), []);
66: }
67: 
68: export function setCachedList(entity, data) {
69:   writeJSON(cacheKey(entity), data);
70: }
71: 
72: function getQueue() {
73:   return readJSON(QUEUE_KEY, []);
74: }
75: 
76: function setQueue(queue) {
77:   writeJSON(QUEUE_KEY, queue);
78: }
79: 
80: function enqueue(operation) {
81:   const queue = getQueue();
82:   queue.push(operation);
83:   setQueue(queue);
84: }
85: 
86: function isNetworkFailure(error) {
87:   return !error?.response;
88: }
89: 
90: function isForbidden(error) {
91:   return error?.response?.status === 403;
92: }
93: 
94: export async function syncQueue() {
95:   const queue = getQueue();
96:   if (!queue.length) return;
97: 
98:   const remaining = [];
99: 
100:   for (const item of queue) {
101:     try {
102:       await api.request({
103:         method: item.method,
104:         url: item.url,
105:         data: item.data,
106:         headers: item.headers,
107:       });
108:     } catch {
109:       remaining.push(item);
110:     }
111:   }
112: 
113:   setQueue(remaining);
114: }
115: 
116: function extractPaginatedList(payload) {
117:   if (Array.isArray(payload)) {
118:     return { data: payload, pagination: null };
119:   }
120:   if (payload && Array.isArray(payload.data)) {
121:     return {
122:       data: payload.data,
123:       pagination: payload.pagination || null,
124:     };
125:   }
126:   return { data: [], pagination: null };
127: }
128: 
129: export async function fetchList(
130:   entity,
131:   { page = 1, perPage = 20, filters = {} } = {},
132: ) {
133:   try {
134:     const response = await api.get(`/${entity}`, {
135:       params: {
136:         page,
137:         per_page: perPage,
138:         ...filters,
139:       },
140:     });
141:     const parsed = extractPaginatedList(response.data);
142:     setCachedList(entity, parsed.data);
143:     return {
144:       data: parsed.data,
145:       pagination: parsed.pagination,
146:       offline: false,
147:       forbidden: false,
148:     };
149:   } catch (error) {
150:     if (isForbidden(error)) {
151:       return {
152:         data: [],
153:         pagination: null,
154:         offline: false,
155:         forbidden: true,
156:       };
157:     }
158:     return {
159:       data: getCachedList(entity),
160:       pagination: null,
161:       offline: true,
162:       forbidden: false,
163:     };
164:   }
165: }
166: 
167: export async function createEntity(entity, payload) {
168:   try {
169:     const response = await api.post(`/${entity}`, payload);
170:     return { data: response.data, queued: false, forbidden: false };
171:   } catch (error) {
172:     if (isForbidden(error)) {
173:       return { data: null, queued: false, forbidden: true };
174:     }
175:     if (!isNetworkFailure(error)) {
176:       throw error;
177:     }
178:     enqueue({ method: "post", url: `/${entity}`, data: payload });
179:     return { data: payload, queued: true, forbidden: false };
180:   }
181: }

195:     return { data: { id, ...payload }, queued: true, forbidden: false };
196:   }

212:   }
213: }

63: async function getApprovedExceptionRequest(
64:   exceptionRequestId,
65:   reservationPayload,
66: ) {
67:   if (!exceptionRequestId) return null;
68:   const row = await db("booking_exception_requests")
69:     .where({ id: exceptionRequestId, status: "approved" })
70:     .first();
71:   if (!row) return null;
72: 
73:   const sameUser = row.user_id === reservationPayload.user_id;
74:   const sameResource = row.resource_id === reservationPayload.resource_id;
75:   const overlaps = intervalsOverlap(
76:     reservationPayload.start_time,
77:     reservationPayload.end_time,
78:     new Date(row.start_time),
79:     new Date(row.end_time),
80:   );
81: 
82:   if (!sameUser || !sameResource || !overlaps) {
83:     return null;
84:   }
85: 
86:   return row;
87: }

89: function ensureManagerActor(ctx) {
90:   if (!isManagerRole(ctx.state.actorRole)) {
91:     ctx.throw(403, "manager role is required");
92:   }
93: }

95: async function enforcePeakHourStandingRestriction({ reservationPayload }) {
96:   const policy = await getActiveStandingPolicy();
97:   if (!isPeakHour(reservationPayload.start_time, policy)) {
98:     return null;
99:   }
100: 
101:   const standing = await recalculateAccountStanding(reservationPayload.user_id);
102:   const lowStanding = standing.score < policy.low_score_threshold;
103:   const noShowRestricted =
104:     standing.attendanceSummary.no_show_count >= policy.no_show_limit;
105: 
106:   if (!lowStanding && !noShowRestricted) {
107:     return null;
108:   }
109: 
110:   const approvedOverride = await findApprovedOverride({
111:     userId: reservationPayload.user_id,
112:     resourceId: reservationPayload.resource_id,
113:     start: reservationPayload.start_time,
114:     end: reservationPayload.end_time,
115:   });
116: 
117:   if (approvedOverride) {
118:     return { policy, standing, approvedOverride };
119:   }
120: 
121:   const reasons = [];
122:   if (lowStanding) {
123:     reasons.push(
124:       `standing score ${standing.score} is below threshold ${policy.low_score_threshold}`,
125:     );
126:   }
127:   if (noShowRestricted) {
128:     reasons.push(
129:       `${standing.attendanceSummary.no_show_count} no-shows in ${policy.lookback_days} days exceeds limit ${policy.no_show_limit}`,
130:     );
131:   }
132: 
133:   const error = new Error(
134:     "peak-hour booking requires manager override due to account standing",
135:   );
136:   error.status = 409;
137:   error.details = [
138:     {
139:       code: "account_standing_restriction",
140:       message:
141:         "This user is blocked from peak-hour booking unless a manager override is approved.",
142:     },
143:     ...reasons.map((text) => ({
144:       code: "account_standing_detail",
145:       message: text,
146:     })),
147:   ];
148:   throw error;
149: }
150: 
151: function parseOverridePayload(payload) {
152:   if (!payload.user_id || !payload.start_time || !payload.end_time) {
153:     const error = new Error("user_id, start_time and end_time are required");
154:     error.status = 400;
155:     throw error;
156:   }
157: 
158:   const start = toDate(payload.start_time);
159:   const end = toDate(payload.end_time);
160:   if (!start || !end || start >= end) {
161:     const error = new Error("invalid override time range");
162:     error.status = 400;
163:     throw error;
164:   }
165: 
166:   return {
167:     user_id: payload.user_id,
168:     resource_id: payload.resource_id || null,
169:     start_time: start,
170:     end_time: end,
171:     reason: payload.reason || null,
172:     status: "approved",
173:   };
174: }

176: function parsePositiveInt(value, fallback) {
177:   const parsed = Number.parseInt(String(value ?? ""), 10);
178:   if (Number.isNaN(parsed) || parsed <= 0) return fallback;
179:   return parsed;
180: }

402: reservationRouter.get(
403:   "/availability",
404:   requirePermission("reservations.read"),
405:   async (ctx) => {
406:     const resourceId = String(ctx.query.resource_id || "").trim();
407:     const dateText = String(ctx.query.date || "").trim();
408:     const durationMinutes = Number(
409:       ctx.query.duration_minutes || THIRTY_MINUTES,
410:     );
411:     const userId = resolveActorScopedUserId(
412:       ctx,
413:       String(ctx.query.user_id || ctx.state.actorUserId || "").trim(),
414:     );
415:     const requestedTime = String(ctx.query.start_time || "").trim();
416: 
417:     if (!resourceId || !dateText || !userId) {
418:       ctx.throw(400, "resource_id, date, and user_id are required");
419:     }
420: 
421:     const resource = await db("resources")
422:       .where({ id: resourceId, is_active: true })
423:       .first();
424:     if (!resource) {
425:       ctx.throw(404, "resource not found or inactive");
426:     }
427: 
428:     const policy = ensurePolicy(resource);
429:     if (durationMinutes < policy.minDurationMinutes) {
430:       ctx.throw(
431:         400,
432:         `minimum duration is ${policy.minDurationMinutes} minutes`,
433:       );
434:     }
435:     if (durationMinutes > policy.maxDurationMinutes) {
436:       ctx.throw(
437:         400,
438:         `maximum duration is ${policy.maxDurationMinutes} minutes`,
439:       );
440:     }
441:     if (durationMinutes % THIRTY_MINUTES !== 0) {
442:       ctx.throw(400, "duration_minutes must be in 30-minute increments");
443:     }
444: 
445:     const dayRule = await getCalendarDayRule(new Date(`${dateText}T00:00:00`));
446:     if (dayRule.is_closed) {
447:       ctx.body = {
448:         resource_id: resourceId,
449:         date: dateText,
450:         duration_minutes: durationMinutes,
451:         day_rule: dayRule,
452:         slots: [],
453:         requested: null,
454:         alternatives: [],
455:       };
456:       return;
457:     }
458: 
459:     const browseStart = parseDateAtTime(
460:       dateText,
461:       dayRule.open_time.slice(0, 5),
462:     );
463:     const browseEnd = parseDateAtTime(dateText, dayRule.close_time.slice(0, 5));
464:     if (!browseStart || !browseEnd) {
465:       ctx.throw(400, "invalid date format");
466:     }
467: 
468:     const queryFrom = addMinutes(browseStart, -15);
469:     const queryTo = addMinutes(browseEnd, 15 + durationMinutes);
470:     const [activeReservations, resourceBlocks, blacklistEntries] =
471:       await Promise.all([
472:         listActiveReservations({
473:           resourceId,
474:           from: queryFrom,
475:           to: queryTo,
476:         }),
477:         listResourceBlocks({
478:           resourceId,
479:           from: queryFrom,
480:           to: queryTo,
481:         }),
482:         listBlacklistEntries({
483:           userId,
484:           resourceId,
485:           from: browseStart,
486:           to: queryTo,
487:         }),
488:       ]);
489: 
490:     const slots = [];
491:     for (
492:       let cursor = new Date(browseStart);
493:       cursor.getTime() + durationMinutes * 60000 <= browseEnd.getTime();
494:       cursor = addMinutes(cursor, THIRTY_MINUTES)
495:     ) {
496:       const start = new Date(cursor);
497:       const end = addMinutes(start, durationMinutes);
498:       const conflicts = detectSchedulingConflicts({
499:         start,
500:         end,
501:         resource,
502:         activeReservations,
503:         resourceBlocks,
504:         blacklistEntries,
505:       });
506: 
507:       slots.push({
508:         start_time: start.toISOString(),
509:         end_time: end.toISOString(),
510:         available: conflicts.length === 0,
511:         conflicts: conflicts.map((item) => item.message),
512:       });
513:     }
514: 
515:     let requested = null;
516:     if (requestedTime) {
517:       const requestedStart = parseDateAtTime(dateText, requestedTime);
518:       if (!requestedStart) {
519:         ctx.throw(400, "invalid start_time format");
520:       }
521:       const requestedEnd = addMinutes(requestedStart, durationMinutes);
522:       const conflicts = detectSchedulingConflicts({
523:         start: requestedStart,
524:         end: requestedEnd,
525:         resource,
526:         activeReservations,
527:         resourceBlocks,
528:         blacklistEntries,
529:       });
530:       requested = {
531:         start_time: requestedStart.toISOString(),
532:         end_time: requestedEnd.toISOString(),
533:         available: conflicts.length === 0,
534:         conflicts: conflicts.map((item) => item.message),
535:       };
536:     }
537: 
538:     const availableSlots = slots.filter((slot) => slot.available);
539:     const alternatives = requested
540:       ? availableSlots
541:           .sort((left, right) => {
542:             const requestedStartMs = new Date(requested.start_time).getTime();
543:             const leftDelta = Math.abs(
544:               new Date(left.start_time).getTime() - requestedStartMs,
545:             );
546:             const rightDelta = Math.abs(
547:               new Date(right.start_time).getTime() - requestedStartMs,
548:             );
549:             return leftDelta - rightDelta;
550:           })
551:           .slice(0, 6)
552:       : availableSlots.slice(0, 6);
553: 
554:     ctx.body = {
555:       resource_id: resourceId,
556:       date: dateText,
557:       duration_minutes: durationMinutes,
558:       day_rule: dayRule,
559:       slots,
560:       requested,
561:       alternatives,
562:     };
563:   },
564: );

985:   async (ctx) => {
986:     const now = new Date();
987:     const candidates = await db("reservations as r")
988:       .join("resources as res", "res.id", "r.resource_id")
989:       .select(
990:         "r.id",
991:         "r.user_id",
992:         "r.start_time",
993:         "res.late_check_in_grace_minutes as late_grace",
994:       )
995:       .where("r.status", "booked");
996: 
997:     const markIds = candidates
998:       .filter((row) => {
999:         const grace = Number(row.late_grace || 15);
1000:         const cutoff = new Date(
1001:           new Date(row.start_time).getTime() + grace * 60000,
1002:         );
1003:         return now > cutoff;
1004:       })
1005:       .map((row) => row.id);
1006: 
1007:     if (markIds.length) {
1008:       await db("reservations").whereIn("id", markIds).update({
1009:         status: "no_show",
1010:         updated_at: now,
1011:       });
1012: 
1013:       const markedRows = candidates.filter((row) => markIds.includes(row.id));
1014:       for (const row of markedRows) {
1015:         await appendAttendanceEvent({
1016:           userId: row.user_id,
1017:           reservationId: row.id,
1018:           eventType: "no_show",
1019:           actorUserId: ctx.state.actorUserId,
1020:           eventTime: now,
1021:         });
1022:       }
1023: 
1024:       const uniqueUsers = [...new Set(markedRows.map((row) => row.user_id))];
1025:       for (const userId of uniqueUsers) {
1026:         await recalculateAccountStanding(userId);
1027:       }
1028:     }
1029:

1031:       entity: "reservations",

1035:       actorUserId: ctx.state.actorUserId,
1036:     });
1037: 
1038:     ctx.body = { marked_count: markIds.length };

264: commerceRouter.post(

269:     const orderId = ctx.params.id;

271:       operation: `order_pay:${orderId}`,

294:           await transitionOrderState({

302:             payload: {
303:               method,
304:               masked_reference: maskedReference,
305:               last_four: lastFour,
306:             },
307:           });
308: 
309:           await trx("order_payments").insert({
310:             id: randomUUID(),
311:             order_id: orderId,
312:             payment_method: method,
313:             amount: order.total_amount,
314:             status: "captured",
315:             masked_reference: maskedReference,
316:             last_four: lastFour,
317:             created_at: now,
318:             updated_at: now,
319:           });
320: 
321:           await trx("orders").where({ id: orderId }).update({
322:             payment_method: method,
323:             paid_at: now,
324:             payment_reference_masked: maskedReference,
325:             payment_last_four: lastFour,
326:             updated_at: now,
327:           });
328: 
329:           await transitionOrderState({
330:             trx,
331:             order,
332:             toState: nextState,
333:             eventType:
334:               nextState === orderStates.ACTIVE
335:                 ? "instant_activation"
336:                 : "awaiting_pickup",
337:             reason:
338:               nextState === orderStates.ACTIVE
339:                 ? "Instant activation completed"
340:                 : "Ready for front-desk pickup",
341:             idempotencyKey,
342:             actorUserId: ctx.state.actorUserId,
343:             payload: {},
344:           });

348:             entryType: "payment",

352:             paymentMethod: method,
353:             shiftKey: String(ctx.request.body?.shift_key || "").trim() || null,
354:             metadata: {
355:               masked_reference: maskedReference,
356:               last_four: lastFour,
357:             },
358:           });
359:         });

361:         await writeAudit({
362:           entity: "orders",
363:           entityId: orderId,
364:           action: "pay",

366:             payment_method: method,
367:             masked_reference: maskedReference,

539:         const groupsMap = new Map();

599:             for (const item of groupItems) {

608:               fulfillment_path: path,
609:               subtotal_amount: subtotal,
610:               discount_amount: discount,
611:               total_amount: total,
612:               state: order.state,
613:             });
614:           }

616:           await trx("orders").where({ id: order.id }).del();

60: function contentContainsKeyword(content, keywordRules) {

63:   return keywordRules.some((rule) => {
64:     const keyword = normalizeText(rule.value).toLowerCase();
65:     return keyword && lower.includes(keyword);

81: async function assertCaptchaIfNeeded({
82:   userId,
83:   settings,
84:   challengeId,
85:   answer,
86:   authorIp,
87:   deviceFingerprint,
88:   isAllowlisted,
89: }) {
90:   if (!settings.captcha_required || isAllowlisted) return;
91: 
92:   const id = normalizeText(challengeId);
93:   const response = normalizeText(answer);
94:   if (!id || !response) {
95:     const error = new Error("captcha challenge and answer are required");
96:     error.status = 400;
97:     throw error;
98:   }
99: 
100:   const challenge = await db("captcha_challenges").where({ id }).first();
101:   if (!challenge) {
102:     const error = new Error("captcha challenge not found");
103:     error.status = 400;
104:     throw error;
105:   }
106: 
107:   if (challenge.solved_at) {
108:     const error = new Error("captcha challenge already used");
109:     error.status = 400;
110:     throw error;
111:   }
112: 
113:   if (new Date(challenge.expires_at) < new Date()) {
114:     const error = new Error("captcha challenge expired");
115:     error.status = 400;
116:     throw error;
117:   }
118: 
119:   if (challenge.user_id && challenge.user_id !== userId) {
120:     const error = new Error("captcha challenge does not match user");
121:     error.status = 400;
122:     throw error;
123:   }
124: 
125:   if (challenge.author_ip && challenge.author_ip !== authorIp) {
126:     const error = new Error("captcha challenge does not match IP");
127:     error.status = 400;
128:     throw error;
129:   }
130: 
131:   if (
132:     challenge.device_fingerprint &&
133:     challenge.device_fingerprint !== deviceFingerprint
134:   ) {
135:     const error = new Error("captcha challenge does not match device");
136:     error.status = 400;
137:     throw error;
138:   }
139: 
140:   if (normalizeText(challenge.expected_answer) !== response) {
141:     const error = new Error("captcha answer is incorrect");
142:     error.status = 400;
143:     throw error;
144:   }
145: 
146:   await db("captcha_challenges")
147:     .where({ id })
148:     .update({ solved_at: new Date() });
149: }

151: async function assertThrottle({
152:   userId,
153:   authorIp,
154:   deviceFingerprint,
155:   settings,
156:   isAllowlisted,
157: }) {
158:   if (isAllowlisted) return;
159:   const cutoff = new Date(Date.now() - 60 * 60000);
160: 
161:   const userCountRow = await db("community_posts")
162:     .count({ count: "id" })
163:     .where({ user_id: userId })
164:     .andWhere("created_at", ">=", cutoff)
165:     .first();
166:   if (Number(userCountRow?.count || 0) >= settings.max_posts_per_hour) {
167:     const error = new Error("post throttle exceeded: max 10 posts per hour");
168:     error.status = 429;
169:     throw error;
170:   }
171: 
172:   if (settings.max_ip_posts_per_hour && authorIp) {
173:     const ipCountRow = await db("community_posts")
174:       .count({ count: "id" })
175:       .where({ author_ip: authorIp })
176:       .andWhere("created_at", ">=", cutoff)
177:       .first();
178:     if (Number(ipCountRow?.count || 0) >= settings.max_ip_posts_per_hour) {
179:       const error = new Error("IP throttle exceeded for posting");
180:       error.status = 429;
181:       throw error;
182:     }
183:   }
184: 
185:   if (settings.max_device_posts_per_hour && deviceFingerprint) {
186:     const deviceCountRow = await db("community_posts")
187:       .count({ count: "id" })
188:       .where({ device_fingerprint: deviceFingerprint })
189:       .andWhere("created_at", ">=", cutoff)
190:       .first();
191:     if (
192:       Number(deviceCountRow?.count || 0) >= settings.max_device_posts_per_hour
193:     ) {
194:       const error = new Error("device throttle exceeded for posting");
195:       error.status = 429;
196:       throw error;
197:     }
198:   }
199: }

212: 
213:     const postsQuery = db("community_posts")

221: 
222:     const posts = await postsQuery.limit(perPage).offset(offset);
223: 
224:     const totalQuery = db("community_posts")
225:       .count({ total: "id" })

227:       .whereNull("parent_post_id");

229:       totalQuery.where({ user_id: authorId });

231:     const totalRow = await totalQuery.first();

238:           .orderBy("created_at", "asc")

240: 
241:     const repliesByParent = replies.reduce((acc, reply) => {
242:       const list = acc[reply.parent_post_id] || [];
243:       list.push(reply);
244:       acc[reply.parent_post_id] = list;
245:       return acc;
246:     }, {});
247: 
248:     ctx.body = {
249:       data: posts.map((post) => ({
250:         ...post,
251:         replies: repliesByParent[post.id] || [],
252:       })),
253:       pagination: {
254:         page,

24: function toConflictMessage(code) {

27:       return "Capacity exceeded for this resource at the selected time.";
28:     case "occupancy_overlap":

34:     case "blacklist_restriction":

107: export function ensurePolicy(resource) {
108:   return {
109:     bookingWindowDays: Number(resource.booking_window_days || 30),
110:     minDurationMinutes: Number(resource.min_duration_minutes || 30),
111:     maxDurationMinutes: Number(resource.max_duration_minutes || 240),
112:     earlyCheckInMinutes: Number(resource.early_check_in_minutes || 10),
113:     lateCheckInGraceMinutes: Number(resource.late_check_in_grace_minutes || 15),
114:     allowSlotStitching: Boolean(resource.allow_slot_stitching),
115:   };

118: export function enforceRuleWindow(start, now, policy) {
119:   const latest = new Date(
120:     now.getTime() + policy.bookingWindowDays * 24 * 60 * 60000,
121:   );
122:   if (start > latest) {
123:     const error = new Error(
124:       `booking exceeds ${policy.bookingWindowDays}-day window`,
125:     );
126:     error.status = 400;
127:     throw error;
128:   }
129: }

131: export function enforceRuleDuration(start, end, policy) {
132:   const minutes = diffMinutes(start, end);
133:   if (minutes < policy.minDurationMinutes) {
134:     const error = new Error(
135:       `minimum duration is ${policy.minDurationMinutes} minutes`,
136:     );
137:     error.status = 400;
138:     throw error;
139:   }
140:   if (minutes > policy.maxDurationMinutes) {
141:     const error = new Error(
142:       `maximum duration is ${policy.maxDurationMinutes} minutes`,
143:     );
144:     error.status = 400;
145:     throw error;
146:   }
147:   if (minutes % THIRTY_MINUTES !== 0) {
148:     const error = new Error("reservation duration must be in 30-minute slots");
149:     error.status = 400;
150:     throw error;
151:   }
152: }

206:     })

216: export function detectSchedulingConflicts({
217:   start,
218:   end,
219:   resource,
220:   activeReservations,
221:   resourceBlocks,
222:   blacklistEntries,
223: }) {
224:   const conflicts = [];
225: 
226:   const overlaps = activeReservations.filter((row) =>
227:     intervalsOverlap(
228:       start,
229:       end,
230:       new Date(row.start_time),
231:       new Date(row.end_time),
232:     ),
233:   );
234:   const capacity = Number(resource.capacity || 1);
235:   if (overlaps.length > 0) {
236:     if (capacity <= 1) {
237:       conflicts.push("occupancy_overlap");
238:     } else if (overlaps.length >= capacity) {
239:       conflicts.push("capacity_exceeded");
240:     }
241:   }
242: 
243:   const bufferMinutes = 15;
244:   const hasCleaningBufferConflict = activeReservations.some((row) => {
245:     const existingStart = new Date(row.start_time);
246:     const existingEnd = new Date(row.end_time);
247:     const expandedStart = new Date(
248:       existingStart.getTime() - bufferMinutes * 60000,
249:     );
250:     const expandedEnd = new Date(existingEnd.getTime() + bufferMinutes * 60000);
251:     const directOverlap = intervalsOverlap(
252:       start,
253:       end,
254:       existingStart,
255:       existingEnd,
256:     );
257:     if (directOverlap) return false;
258:     return intervalsOverlap(start, end, expandedStart, expandedEnd);
259:   });
260:   if (hasCleaningBufferConflict) {
261:     conflicts.push("cleaning_buffer");
262:   }
263: 
264:   const hasBlockedResource = resourceBlocks.some((row) =>
265:     intervalsOverlap(
266:       start,
267:       end,
268:       new Date(row.start_time),
269:       new Date(row.end_time),
270:     ),
271:   );
272:   if (hasBlockedResource) {
273:     conflicts.push("blocked_resource");
274:   }
275: 
276:   const hasBlacklistRestriction = blacklistEntries.some((row) => {
277:     const blockedFrom = new Date(row.blocked_from);
278:     const blockedUntil = row.blocked_until
279:       ? new Date(row.blocked_until)
280:       : new Date("9999-12-31T23:59:59.999Z");
281:     return intervalsOverlap(start, end, blockedFrom, blockedUntil);
282:   });
283:   if (hasBlacklistRestriction) {
284:     conflicts.push("blacklist_restriction");
285:   }
286: 
287:   return [...new Set(conflicts)].map((code) => ({
288:     code,
289:     message: toConflictMessage(code),
290:   }));
291: }

322: export async function enforceOperatingHours(start, end, db = defaultDb) {

326:       `resource is unavailable on ${dayRule.date} (${dayRule.holiday_name || "holiday"})`,
327:     );
328:     error.status = 409;
329:     error.details = [
330:       {

333:       },

339:   const closeAt = parseClockOnDate(start, dayRule.close_time);
340:   if (start < openAt || end > closeAt) {
341:     const label =
342:       dayRule.source === "holiday" ? "holiday schedule" : "operating hours";
343:     const error = new Error(
344:       `reservation is outside ${label} (${dayRule.open_time} - ${dayRule.close_time})`,
345:     );
346:     error.status = 409;
347:     error.details = [
348:       {
349:         code: "outside_operating_hours",
350:         message: `Booking must be within ${dayRule.open_time} and ${dayRule.close_time}.`,
351:       },
352:     ];
353:     throw error;
354:   }

356:   return dayRule;

359: export async function validateWithResource({
360:   reservationPayload,
361:   ignoreReservationId = null,
362:   db = defaultDb,
363: }) {
364:   const resource = await db("resources")
365:     .where({ id: reservationPayload.resource_id, is_active: true })
366:     .first();
367:   if (!resource) {
368:     const error = new Error("resource not found or inactive");
369:     error.status = 400;
370:     throw error;
371:   }
372: 
373:   const policy = ensurePolicy(resource);
374:   const now = new Date();
375: 
376:   await enforceOperatingHours(
377:     reservationPayload.start_time,
378:     reservationPayload.end_time,
379:     db,
380:   );
381: 
382:   enforceRuleWindow(reservationPayload.start_time, now, policy);
383:   enforceRuleDuration(
384:     reservationPayload.start_time,
385:     reservationPayload.end_time,
386:     policy,
387:   );
388: 
389:   const from = new Date(reservationPayload.start_time.getTime() - 15 * 60000);
390:   const to = new Date(reservationPayload.end_time.getTime() + 15 * 60000);
391:   const [activeReservations, resourceBlocks, blacklistEntries] =
392:     await Promise.all([
393:       listActiveReservations({
394:         resourceId: reservationPayload.resource_id,
395:         from,
396:         to,
397:         ignoreReservationId,
398:         db,
399:       }),
400:       listResourceBlocks({
401:         resourceId: reservationPayload.resource_id,
402:         from,
403:         to,
404:         db,
405:       }),
406:       listBlacklistEntries({
407:         userId: reservationPayload.user_id,
408:         resourceId: reservationPayload.resource_id,
409:         from: reservationPayload.start_time,
410:         to: reservationPayload.end_time,
411:         db,
412:       }),
413:     ]);
414: 
415:   const conflicts = detectSchedulingConflicts({
416:     start: reservationPayload.start_time,
417:     end: reservationPayload.end_time,
418:     resource,
419:     activeReservations,
420:     resourceBlocks,
421:     blacklistEntries,
422:   });
423: 
424:   if (conflicts.length) {
425:     const error = new Error(conflicts[0].message);
426:     error.status = 409;
427:     error.details = conflicts;
428:     throw error;
429:   }
430: 
431:   return { resource, policy };
432: }

434: export async function stitchIfNeeded({
435:   reservation,
436:   policy,
437:   actorUserId,
438:   writeAudit,
439:   db = defaultDb,
440: }) {
441:   if (!policy.allowSlotStitching) return reservation;
442:   if (!isThirtyMinuteSlot(reservation)) return reservation;
443: 
444:   const candidates = await db("reservations")
445:     .where({
446:       resource_id: reservation.resource_id,
447:       user_id: reservation.user_id,
448:     })
449:     .whereIn("status", ["booked"])
450:     .orderBy("start_time", "asc");
451: 
452:   const chain = candidates.filter((row) => {
453:     const startsOrEndsInside =
454:       new Date(row.end_time).getTime() >=
455:         new Date(reservation.start_time).getTime() - THIRTY_MINUTES * 60000 &&
456:       new Date(row.start_time).getTime() <=
457:         new Date(reservation.end_time).getTime() + THIRTY_MINUTES * 60000;
458:     return startsOrEndsInside;
459:   });
460: 
461:   if (chain.length <= 1) return reservation;
462: 
463:   const sorted = [...chain].sort(compareByStartTime);
464:   for (let index = 1; index < sorted.length; index += 1) {
465:     if (
466:       !isThirtyMinuteSlot(sorted[index - 1]) ||
467:       !isThirtyMinuteSlot(sorted[index])
468:     ) {
469:       return reservation;
470:     }
471:     if (!areAdjacent(sorted[index - 1], sorted[index])) {
472:       return reservation;
473:     }
474:   }
475: 
476:   const stitchedStart = new Date(sorted[0].start_time);
477:   const stitchedEnd = new Date(sorted[sorted.length - 1].end_time);
478:   const stitchedDuration = diffMinutes(stitchedStart, stitchedEnd);
479:   if (stitchedDuration > policy.maxDurationMinutes) {
480:     return reservation;
481:   }
482: 
483:   const keeper = sorted[0];
484:   const removeIds = sorted.slice(1).map((item) => item.id);
485: 
486:   await db.transaction(async (trx) => {
487:     await trx("reservations").where({ id: keeper.id }).update({
488:       start_time: stitchedStart,
489:       end_time: stitchedEnd,
490:       updated_at: new Date(),
491:     });
492: 
493:     if (removeIds.length) {
494:       await trx("reservations").whereIn("id", removeIds).del();
495:     }
496:   });
497: 
498:   if (typeof writeAudit === "function") {
499:     await writeAudit({
500:       entity: "reservations",
501:       entityId: keeper.id,
502:       action: "stitch",
503:       payload: {
504:         keeper_id: keeper.id,
505:         removed_ids: removeIds,
506:         start_time: stitchedStart,
507:         end_time: stitchedEnd,
508:       },
509:       actorUserId,
510:     });
511:   }
512: 
513:   return db("reservations").where({ id: keeper.id }).first();
514: }

</content>

<content>1: import { describe, expect, test, beforeEach } from "vitest";
2: import { AUTH_TOKEN_KEY } from "../services/api.js";
3: import { installRouteGuards } from "../router.js";
4: 
5: function makeRouterStub() {
6:   const stub = {
7:     guard: null,
8:     beforeEach(handler) {
9:       this.guard = handler;
10:     },
11:   };
12:   installRouteGuards(stub);
13:   return stub;
14: }
15: 
16: describe("Route guard behavior", () => {
17:   beforeEach(() => {
18:     localStorage.clear();
19:   });
20: 
21:   test("redirects unauthenticated users to login", () => {
22:     const router = makeRouterStub();
23:     const result = router.guard({ path: "/checkout", meta: { permission: "orders.read" } });
24:     expect(result).toBe("/login");
25:   });
26: 
27:   test("redirects authenticated users away from login", () => {
28:     localStorage.setItem(AUTH_TOKEN_KEY, "token-1");
29:     const router = makeRouterStub();
30:     const result = router.guard({ path: "/login", meta: {} });
31:     expect(result).toBe("/");
32:   });
33: 
34:   test("sends user to access denied when role lacks route permission", () => {
35:     localStorage.setItem(AUTH_TOKEN_KEY, "token-1");
36:     localStorage.setItem("studio-active-role", "member");
37:     const router = makeRouterStub();
38:     const result = router.guard({
39:       path: "/moderation",
40:       meta: { permission: "community.moderate" },
41:     });
42:     expect(result).toBe("/access-denied");
43:   });
44: });

<content>1: export const roles = {
2:   MEMBER: "member",
3:   FRONT_DESK: "front-desk",
4:   MANAGER: "manager",
5:   MODERATOR: "moderator",
6:   AUDITOR: "auditor",
7: };
8: 
9: export const roleOptions = [
10:   { value: roles.MEMBER, label: "Member" },
11:   { value: roles.FRONT_DESK, label: "Front Desk Staff" },
12:   { value: roles.MANAGER, label: "Studio Manager" },
13:   { value: roles.MODERATOR, label: "Moderator" },
14:   { value: roles.AUDITOR, label: "Auditor" },
15: ];
16: 
17: const rolePermissions = {
18:   [roles.MEMBER]: [
19:     "resources.read",
20:     "reservations.read",
21:     "reservations.write",
22:     "orders.read",
23:     "orders.write",
24:     "refunds.read",
25:     "community.read",
26:     "community.write",
27:     "community.report",
28:   ],
29:   [roles.FRONT_DESK]: [
30:     "users.read",
31:     "users.write",
32:     "resources.read",
33:     "reservations.read",
34:     "reservations.write",
35:     "orders.read",
36:     "orders.write",
37:     "refunds.read",
38:     "refunds.write",
39:     "community.read",
40:     "community.report",
41:   ],
42:   [roles.MANAGER]: [
43:     "users.read",
44:     "users.write",
45:     "resources.read",
46:     "resources.write",
47:     "reservations.read",
48:     "reservations.write",
49:     "orders.read",
50:     "orders.write",
51:     "refunds.read",
52:     "refunds.write",
53:     "audit.read",
54:     "reports.financial",
55:     "reports.security",
56:     "reports.community",
57:     "community.read",
58:     "community.write",
59:     "community.report",
60:     "community.moderate",
61:     "security.permissions.manage",
62:   ],
63:   [roles.MODERATOR]: [
64:     "users.read",
65:     "resources.read",
66:     "reservations.read",
67:     "orders.read",
68:     "refunds.read",
69:     "reports.community",
70:     "community.read",
71:     "community.write",
72:     "community.report",
73:     "community.moderate",
74:   ],
75:   [roles.AUDITOR]: [
76:     "orders.read",
77:     "refunds.read",
78:     "audit.read",
79:     "reports.financial",
80:     "reports.security",
81:     "community.read",
82:   ],
83: };
84: 
85: const roleStorageKey = "studio-active-role";
86: 
87: export function getRoleLabel(role) {
88:   const option = roleOptions.find((item) => item.value === role);
89:   return option ? option.label : "Member";
90: }
91: 
92: export function getActiveRole() {
93:   const value = localStorage.getItem(roleStorageKey);
94:   if (!value || !roleOptions.some((item) => item.value === value)) {
95:     return roles.MEMBER;
96:   }
97:   return value;
98: }
99: 
100: export function setActiveRole(role) {
101:   if (!roleOptions.some((item) => item.value === role)) return;
102:   localStorage.setItem(roleStorageKey, role);
103: }
104: 
105: export function getPermissionsForRole(role) {
106:   return rolePermissions[role] || rolePermissions[roles.MEMBER];
107: }
108: 
109: export function hasPermission(role, permission) {
110:   return getPermissionsForRole(role).includes(permission);
111: }

389:   const page = parsePositiveInt(ctx.query.page, 1);
390:   const perPage = Math.min(parsePositiveInt(ctx.query.per_page, 20), 100);
391:   const offset = (page - 1) * perPage;
392:   const entity = String(ctx.query.entity || "").trim();
393:   const action = String(ctx.query.action || "").trim();
394:   const actorUserId = String(ctx.query.actor_user_id || "").trim();
395: 
396:   const query = db("audit_trails").orderBy("created_at", "desc");
397:   const countQuery = db("audit_trails").count({ total: "id" });
398:   if (entity) {
399:     query.where({ entity });
400:     countQuery.where({ entity });
401:   }
402:   if (action) {
403:     query.where({ action });
404:     countQuery.where({ action });
405:   }
406:   if (actorUserId) {
407:     query.where({ actor_user_id: actorUserId });
408:     countQuery.where({ actor_user_id: actorUserId });
409:   }
410: 
411:   const [{ total = 0 } = { total: 0 }, rows] = await Promise.all([
412:     countQuery,
413:     query.limit(perPage).offset(offset),
414:   ]);
415: 
416:   ctx.body = {
417:     data: rows,
418:     pagination: {
419:       page,

421:       total: Number(total || 0),
422:     },
423:   };

52: export function extractLastFour(text) {
53:   const digits = String(text || "").replace(/\D/g, "");
54:   if (!digits) return null;
55:   return digits.slice(-4).padStart(4, "0");
56: }
57: 
58: export function maskReference(text) {
59:   const cleaned = String(text || "").trim();
60:   if (!cleaned) return null;
61:   if (cleaned.length <= 4) return `****${cleaned}`;
62:   return `${"*".repeat(cleaned.length - 4)}${cleaned.slice(-4)}`;
63: }
64: 
65: export function isCouponActive(coupon) {
66:   if (!coupon || !parseBoolean(coupon.is_active)) return false;
67:   const now = new Date();
68:   if (coupon.starts_at && new Date(coupon.starts_at) > now) return false;
69:   if (coupon.ends_at && new Date(coupon.ends_at) < now) return false;
70:   return true;
71: }
72: 
73: export function buildEligibility(coupon, lines, subtotalAmount) {
74:   const eligibleLines = coupon.applies_to_category
75:     ? lines.filter((line) => line.category === coupon.applies_to_category)
76:     : [...lines];
77:   const eligibleSubtotal = toMoney(
78:     eligibleLines.reduce((sum, line) => sum + line.subtotal_amount, 0),
79:   );
80:   const thresholdBase = coupon.applies_to_category
81:     ? eligibleSubtotal
82:     : subtotalAmount;
83:   const meetsMin = thresholdBase >= toNumber(coupon.min_subtotal);
84:   return { eligibleLines, eligibleSubtotal, meetsMin };
85: }
86: 
87: export function computeCouponDiscount(coupon, eligibleSubtotal) {
88:   const value = toNumber(coupon.discount_value);
89:   let discount =
90:     coupon.discount_type === "fixed" ? value : eligibleSubtotal * (value / 100);
91:   if (coupon.max_discount !== null && coupon.max_discount !== undefined) {
92:     discount = Math.min(discount, toNumber(coupon.max_discount));
93:   }
94:   return toMoney(Math.min(discount, eligibleSubtotal));
95: }
96: 
97: export function distributeDiscount(lines, eligibleLines, totalDiscount) {
98:   const base = toMoney(
99:     eligibleLines.reduce((sum, line) => sum + line.subtotal_amount, 0),
100:   );
101:   if (!base || !totalDiscount) return lines;
102: 
103:   let assigned = 0;
104:   const eligibleSet = new Set(eligibleLines.map((line) => line.line_key));
105:   const lastEligibleKey = eligibleLines[eligibleLines.length - 1]?.line_key;
106:   return lines.map((line) => {
107:     if (!eligibleSet.has(line.line_key)) {
108:       return {
109:         ...line,
110:         discount_amount: 0,
111:         total_amount: line.subtotal_amount,
112:       };
113:     }
114:     const isLast = line.line_key === lastEligibleKey;
115:     const proportional = toMoney((line.subtotal_amount / base) * totalDiscount);
116:     const discount = isLast
117:       ? toMoney(totalDiscount - assigned)
118:       : Math.min(proportional, line.subtotal_amount);
119:     assigned = toMoney(assigned + discount);
120:     return {
121:       ...line,
122:       discount_amount: discount,
123:       total_amount: toMoney(line.subtotal_amount - discount),
124:     };
125:   });
126: }

<content>1: import { createHash, randomUUID } from "crypto";
2: import { db } from "../db.js";
3: 
4: export function stableStringify(value) {
5:   if (value === null || typeof value !== "object") {
6:     return JSON.stringify(value);
7:   }
8:   if (Array.isArray(value)) {
9:     return `[${value.map((item) => stableStringify(item)).join(",")}]`;
10:   }
11:   const keys = Object.keys(value).sort();
12:   return `{${keys
13:     .map((key) => `${JSON.stringify(key)}:${stableStringify(value[key])}`)
14:     .join(",")}}`;
15: }
16: 
17: export function computeChainHash({ previousHash, payload }) {
18:   return createHash("sha256")
19:     .update(String(previousHash || "GENESIS"))
20:     .update("|")
21:     .update(stableStringify(payload))
22:     .digest("hex");
23: }
24: 
25: export async function appendFinancialLog({
26:   trx = null,
27:   entryType,
28:   referenceType,
29:   referenceId,
30:   amount,
31:   paymentMethod = null,
32:   shiftKey = null,
33:   metadata = {},
34: }) {
35:   const runner = trx || db;
36:   const previous = await runner("financial_logs")
37:     .orderBy("created_at", "desc")
38:     .orderBy("id", "desc")
39:     .first();
40:   const previousHash = previous?.entry_hash || null;
41:   const now = new Date();
42: 
43:   const payload = {
44:     entry_type: entryType,
45:     reference_type: referenceType,
46:     reference_id: referenceId,
47:     amount: Number(amount || 0),
48:     payment_method: paymentMethod,
49:     shift_key: shiftKey,
50:     metadata,
51:     created_at: now.toISOString(),
52:   };
53:   const entryHash = computeChainHash({ previousHash, payload });
54: 
55:   const row = {
56:     id: randomUUID(),
57:     entry_type: entryType,
58:     reference_type: referenceType,
59:     reference_id: referenceId,
60:     amount: Number(amount || 0),
61:     payment_method: paymentMethod,
62:     shift_key: shiftKey,
63:     metadata_json: JSON.stringify(metadata || {}),
64:     previous_hash: previousHash,
65:     entry_hash: entryHash,
66:     created_at: now,
67:   };
68: 
69:   await runner("financial_logs").insert(row);
70:   return row;
71: }

<type>file</type>
<content>1: # Fitness and Wellness Studio Platform (Local Network)
2: 
3: This is the first implementation slice for the core platform architecture:
4: 
5: - Vue.js front end (`frontend/`) that consumes local REST APIs
6: - Koa backend (`backend/`) exposing API endpoints for users, resources, reservations, orders, refunds, and audit trail reads
7: - MySQL as the system of record via Knex migrations
8: - Offline-first behavior in the front end using local cache + queued write operations
9: - Role-based dashboards and access control for Members, Front Desk Staff, Studio Managers, Moderators, and Auditors
10: 
11: ## Start Command (How to Run)
12: 
13: Use one command from the `repo` directory:
14: 
15: ```bash
16: docker compose up
17: ```
18: 
19: This single command starts all declared dependencies (MySQL, backend, frontend), waits for database readiness, runs migrations automatically, and starts the app services.
20: 
21: For Docker first-run, bootstrap also runs automatically and seeds:
22: 
23: - baseline resources (`Studio Room A`, `Studio Room B`)
24: - one manager account (`manager@local.test`)
25: 
26: Default local bootstrap password in Compose is `LocalManagerPass123!` and should be changed after first login in real environments.
27: 
28: ## Service Address (Services List)
29: 
30: - Frontend (Vue): `http://localhost:5173`
31: - Backend API (Koa): `http://localhost:4000/api/v1`
32: - MySQL: `localhost:3306`
33: 
34: ## Project Structure
35: 
36: ```text
37: repo/
38: Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ backend/
39: Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ migrations/
40: Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ src/
41: Γöé   ΓööΓöÇΓöÇ tests/
42: Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ frontend/
43: Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ src/
44: Γöé   ΓööΓöÇΓöÇ dist/
45: Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ API_tests/
46: Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ unit_tests/
47: Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ docker-compose.yml
48: ΓööΓöÇΓöÇ run_tests.sh
49: ```
50: 
51: ## Running Tests
52: 
53: From the `repo` directory:
54: 
55: ```bash
56: ./run_tests.sh
57: ```
58: 
59: This script is idempotent, non-interactive, and runs tests in the backend Docker container (Node 20) via `docker compose exec -T backend ...`.
60: 
61: Behavior:
62: 
63: - If Compose services are not running, it starts them automatically.
64: - It runs all unit tests from `unit_tests/`.
65: - It runs all API tests from `API_tests/`.
66: - If Docker Compose is unavailable, it falls back to host execution only when host Node is `20+`; otherwise it fails fast with a clear message.
67: 
68: It runs:
69: 
70: - all unit tests from `unit_tests/`
71: - all API tests from `API_tests/`
72: 
73: ## Verification Guide (Core Features)
74: 
75: 1. Run `docker compose up` from `repo/` and wait until all three services are healthy/running.
76: 2. Open `http://localhost:5173` and confirm the frontend loads.
77: 3. Open `http://localhost:4000/api/v1/health` and confirm the response includes `"status":"ok"`.
78: 4. Verify a protected route returns standard auth errors, for example call `GET /api/v1/me/dashboard` without a token and confirm `401` JSON response.
79: 5. Verify RBAC behavior by logging in as different roles and confirming allowed/forbidden actions in dashboard/API.
80: 6. Run `./run_tests.sh` and confirm all unit + API tests pass.
81: 
82: ## Optional Local (Non-Docker) Setup
83: 
84: ### 1) Install dependencies
85: 
86: ```bash
87: npm install
88: ```
89: 
90: ### 2) Configure environment
91: 
92: **Linux / macOS:**
93: 
94: ```bash
95: cp backend/.env.example backend/.env
96: cp frontend/.env.example frontend/.env
97: ```
98: 
99: **Windows (Command Prompt):**
100: 
101: ```bash
102: copy backend\.env.example backend\.env
103: copy frontend\.env.example frontend\.env
104: ```
105: 
106: Update values in `backend/.env` for your local MySQL instance.
107: 
108: ### 3) Create database and run migrations
109: 
110: Create the database manually (example name: `studio_local`) and run:
111: 
112: ```bash
113: npm run migrate
114: ```
115: 
116: ### 3.1) Run one-time bootstrap (required on clean DB)
117: 
118: Set bootstrap env values and run:
119: 
120: ```bash
121: # Linux/macOS
122: BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_EMAIL=manager@local.test \
123: BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD='ChooseAStrongPassword!' \
124: npm run bootstrap --workspace backend
125: ```
126: 
127: ```powershell
128: # Windows PowerShell
129: $env:BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_EMAIL = "manager@local.test"
130: $env:BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD = "ChooseAStrongPassword!"
131: npm run bootstrap --workspace backend
132: ```
133: 
134: The bootstrap script is idempotent and safe to re-run.
135: 
136: ### 4) Start services (two terminals)
137: 
138: Terminal 1:
139: 
140: ```bash
141: npm run dev:backend
142: ```
143: 
144: Terminal 2:
145: 
146: ```bash
147: npm run dev:frontend
148: ```
149: 
150: ### API base
151: 
152: - Backend base URL: `http://<local-ip>:4000/api/v1`
153: - Frontend dev URL: `http://<local-ip>:5173`
154: 
155: For LAN usage, keep backend `HOST=0.0.0.0` and Vite server host is already `0.0.0.0`.
156: 
157: ## Role and access behavior
158: 
159: - Frontend role profile selector in the sidebar switches dashboard views and available routes.
160: - Backend enforces RBAC from authenticated Bearer sessions (`Authorization: Bearer <token>`).
161: - Protected endpoints require a valid session token and return `401` when missing/invalid/expired.
162: - Public endpoints are limited to `GET /api/v1/health`, `POST /api/v1/auth/register`, and `POST /api/v1/auth/login`.
163: - Dashboard metadata endpoint: `GET /api/v1/me/dashboard`.
164: 
165: ## Resource and reservation policy rules
166: 
167: - Resources now store configurable policy fields:
168:   - `booking_window_days` (default `30`)
169:   - `min_duration_minutes` (default `30`)
170:   - `max_duration_minutes` (default `240`)
171:   - `early_check_in_minutes` (default `10`)
172:   - `late_check_in_grace_minutes` (default `15`)
173:   - `allow_slot_stitching` (default `true`)
174: - Reservation API enforces booking window, duration limits, 30-minute slot increments, and overlap prevention.
175: - Adjacent 30-minute reservations for the same member/resource are stitched into one continuous reservation when policy allows.
176: - Check-in endpoint: `POST /api/v1/reservations/:id/check-in`.
177: - No-show batch endpoint: `POST /api/v1/reservations/mark-no-shows`.
178: 
179: ## Availability and conflict detection
180: 
181: - Availability browse endpoint: `GET /api/v1/availability` with query params:
182:   - `resource_id`
183:   - `user_id`
184:   - `date` (`YYYY-MM-DD`)
185:   - `duration_minutes` (30-minute increments)
186:   - optional `start_time` (`HH:mm`)
187: - Conflict explanations are returned in plain language for:
188:   - capacity exceeded
189:   - occupancy overlap
190:   - required 15-minute cleaning buffer
191:   - blocked resource
192:   - blacklist restriction
193: - Alternative slot suggestions are returned with each availability response.
194: - Additional policy entities:
195:   - `resource-blocks`
196:   - `reservation-blacklists`
197: 
198: ## Account standing and booking restrictions
199: 
200: - Users have internal standing score (`0-100`) in `users.standing_score`.
201: - Standing is recalculated from `attendance_history` using configurable policy in
202:   `account_standing_policies`.
203: - Peak-hour restrictions apply when either condition is true:
204:   - standing score below threshold (default `< 60`)
205:   - no-shows in lookback window reach limit (default `>= 2` in 30 days)
206: - Restricted peak-hour bookings require approved manager override from
207:   `reservation_overrides`.
208: - API endpoints:
209:   - `GET /api/v1/account-standing-policy`
210:   - `PUT /api/v1/account-standing-policy/:id` (manager)
211:   - `GET /api/v1/account-standing/:userId`
212:   - `GET /api/v1/attendance-history?user_id=...`
213:   - `GET /api/v1/reservation-overrides`
214:   - `POST /api/v1/reservation-overrides` (manager)
215: 
216: ## Calendar, holidays, and booking exceptions
217: 
218: - Holiday rules are stored in `holiday_rules` and editable at `/holiday-rules` in the UI.
219: - Calendar day rule endpoint: `GET /api/v1/calendar/day-rules?date=YYYY-MM-DD`.
220: - Availability view now surfaces holiday context and reduced-hour windows.
221: - Exception workflow for rule overrides:
222:   - Member/staff submits request with reason: `POST /api/v1/booking-exceptions`
223:   - Manager decides with required justification: `POST /api/v1/booking-exceptions/:id/decision`
224:   - List requests: `GET /api/v1/booking-exceptions`
225: - Reservation create/update accepts `exception_request_id`; approved matching exceptions can bypass standard rule conflicts.
226: - All exception requests and decisions are logged to `audit_trails` with reason text.
227: 
228: ## Unified cart, pricing, coupons, and fulfillment split/merge
229: 
230: - Commerce catalog endpoint: `GET /api/v1/commerce/catalog`.
231: - Coupon list endpoint: `GET /api/v1/commerce/coupons`.
232: - Real-time cart quote endpoint: `POST /api/v1/commerce/cart/quote`.
233:   - Supports standard catalog `items` and `reservation_lines` in one payload.
234: - Checkout endpoint with split/merge mode: `POST /api/v1/commerce/checkout`.
235:   - `split_mode=auto_split` creates separate orders by fulfillment path.
236:   - `split_mode=merge_all` creates one merged order.
237: - Post-checkout operations:
238:   - `POST /api/v1/commerce/orders/:id/split`
239:   - `POST /api/v1/commerce/orders/merge`
240: - Coupon rules support fixed and percentage discounts with:
241:   - minimum subtotal threshold
242:   - category targeting (e.g., class packs)
243:   - max discount cap (e.g., 15% max $25)
244: - Seeded examples include:
245:   - `$10 off purchases over $75` (`SAVE10OVER75`)
246:   - `15% off class pack, max $25` (`CLASSPACK15`)
247: 
248: Availability-to-checkout flow:
249: 
250: - From `Availability Browser`, create a reservation from an available requested slot.
251: - This stores a checkout draft reservation line.
252: - Open `Unified Cart & Checkout` to see the reservation line merged with catalog items.
253: 
254: ## Order state machine, idempotency, and local payments
255: 
256: - Orders now use explicit state transitions:
257:   - `pending_payment -> paid -> active|awaiting_pickup -> fulfilled`
258:   - `pending_payment -> cancelled|expired`
259: - Client idempotency keys are required for write operations (checkout/pay/cancel/transition/split/merge).
260:   - Provide via `x-idempotency-key` header or `idempotency_key` in request body.
261: - Unpaid orders auto-expire after 15 minutes using:
262:   - automatic background worker sweep (runs every minute by default)
263:   - optional manual trigger: `POST /api/v1/commerce/orders/expire-unpaid`
264:   - both paths release inventory/reservation holds and are idempotent.
265: - Supported local payment methods only:
266:   - `cash`
267:   - `card_terminal`
268:   - `gift_certificate`
269: - Manual payment references are stored only as masked text and `last_four`.
270: - Related persistence tables:
271:   - `idempotency_keys`
272:   - `order_state_events`
273:   - `order_holds`
274:   - `order_payments`
275: 
276: ## Community feed and moderation
277: 
278: - Community feed endpoints:
279:   - `GET /api/v1/community/feed`
280:   - `POST /api/v1/community/posts` (supports replies via `parent_post_id`)
281:   - `POST /api/v1/community/posts/:id/report`
282:   - `GET /api/v1/community/reports/mine`
283: - Moderation endpoints:
284:   - `GET /api/v1/community/moderation/queue`
285:   - `POST /api/v1/community/moderation/posts/:id/decision`
286:   - `POST /api/v1/community/moderation/reports/:id/decision`
287: - Anti-abuse controls:
288:   - account throttle: max posts/hour (default 10)
289:   - optional IP/device throttle limits
290:   - local CAPTCHA challenges via `POST /api/v1/community/captcha/challenge`
291:   - allowlist/blocklist rules (`community-rules` table)
292:   - temporary/permanent bans (`community-bans` table)
293: - Report statuses are visible to reporters via `community/reports/mine`.
294: 
295: ## Security, audit, and reconciliation
296: 
297: - Authentication now supports salted password hashing (`scrypt`) via:
298:   - `POST /api/v1/auth/register`
299:   - `POST /api/v1/auth/login`
300: - Repeated login failures trigger lockout:
301:   - account locked for 15 minutes after 8 failed attempts
302:   - events written to queryable `security_events`
303: - Sensitive APIs support optional anti-replay request timestamps using
304:   `x-request-timestamp` within configured window.
305: - Permission changes are tracked with complete audit trail:
306:   - only manager role with `security.permissions.manage` may update permissions
307:   - self-grant of privileged permissions is explicitly denied
308:   - `PUT /api/v1/security/users/:id/permissions`
309: - Fine issuance and refund processing are auditable and financial-log backed:
310:   - `POST /api/v1/fines`
311:   - `POST /api/v1/refunds/:id/process`
312: - Financial logs are tamper-evident with chained hashes in `financial_logs`.
313: - Reconciliation APIs:
314:   - `GET /api/v1/reconciliation/daily?date=YYYY-MM-DD`
315:   - `POST /api/v1/reconciliation/shift-close`
316:   - `GET /api/v1/reconciliation/shift/:shiftKey`
317: - Variance flag is raised when drawer counted total differs from expected by more than `$5.00`.

(End of file - total 317 lines)
</content>

This is the first implementation slice for the core platform architecture:

- Vue.js front end (`frontend/`) that consumes local REST APIs
- Koa backend (`backend/`) exposing API endpoints for users, resources, reservations, orders, refunds, and audit trail reads

- Offline-first behavior in the front end using local cache + queued write operations
- Role-based dashboards and access control for Members, Front Desk Staff, Studio Managers, Moderators, and Auditors

## Start Command (How to Run)

Use one command from the `repo` directory:

```bash
docker compose up
```

This single command starts all declared dependencies (MySQL, backend, frontend), waits for database readiness, runs migrations automatically, and starts the app services.

*** Update File: C:\Users\user\Desktop\eaglepoint_new\Week-1\TASK-46\repo\README.md
@@
 - Variance flag is raised when drawer counted total differs from expected by more than `$5.00`.
+
+## Remaining scope exclusions and limitations
+
+- CAPTCHA is a local arithmetic challenge designed for audit/testing; it is not a production anti-bot service.
+- Tamper-evident verification in the frontend flags hash-chain continuity issues from retrieved rows, but does not recompute server-side payload hashes in-browser.
+- Real-time dashboards and audit browsers are polling/refresh-based; no websocket push channel is implemented in this slice.
+- Some moderation/security settings are surfaced read-only for lower-privilege roles due RBAC constraints.
*** End Patch"}

# Fitness and Wellness Studio Platform (Local Network)

This is the first implementation slice for the core platform architecture:

- Vue.js front end (`frontend/`) that consumes local REST APIs
- Koa backend (`backend/`) exposing API endpoints for users, resources, reservations, orders, refunds, and audit trail reads
- MySQL as the system of record via Knex migrations
- Offline-first behavior in the front end using local cache + queued write operations
- Role-based dashboards and access control for Members, Front Desk Staff, Studio Managers, Moderators, and Auditors

## Start Command (How to Run)

Use one command from the `repo` directory:

```bash
docker compose up
```

This single command starts all declared dependencies (MySQL, backend, frontend), waits for database readiness, runs migrations automatically, and starts the app services.

For Docker first-run, bootstrap also runs automatically and seeds:

- baseline resources (`Studio Room A`, `Studio Room B`)
- one manager account (`manager@local.test`)

Default local bootstrap password in Compose is `LocalManagerPass123!` and should be changed after first login in real environments.

## Service Address (Services List)

- Frontend (Vue): `http://localhost:5173`
- Backend API (Koa): `http://localhost:4000/api/v1`
- MySQL: `localhost:3306`

## Project Structure

```text
repo/
Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ backend/
Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ migrations/
Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ src/
Γöé   ΓööΓöÇΓöÇ tests/
Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ frontend/
Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ src/
Γöé   ΓööΓöÇΓöÇ dist/
Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ API_tests/
Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ unit_tests/
Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ docker-compose.yml
ΓööΓöÇΓöÇ run_tests.sh
```

## Running Tests

From the `repo` directory:

```bash
./run_tests.sh
```

This script is idempotent, non-interactive, and runs tests in the backend Docker container (Node 20) via `docker compose exec -T backend ...`.

Behavior:

- If Compose services are not running, it starts them automatically.
- It runs all unit tests from `unit_tests/`.
- It runs all API tests from `API_tests/`.
- If Docker Compose is unavailable, it falls back to host execution only when host Node is `20+`; otherwise it fails fast with a clear message.

It runs:

- all unit tests from `unit_tests/`
- all API tests from `API_tests/`

## Verification Guide (Core Features)

1. Run `docker compose up` from `repo/` and wait until all three services are healthy/running.
2. Open `http://localhost:5173` and confirm the frontend loads.
3. Open `http://localhost:4000/api/v1/health` and confirm the response includes `"status":"ok"`.
4. Verify a protected route returns standard auth errors, for example call `GET /api/v1/me/dashboard` without a token and confirm `401` JSON response.
5. Verify RBAC behavior by logging in as different roles and confirming allowed/forbidden actions in dashboard/API.
6. Run `./run_tests.sh` and confirm all unit + API tests pass.

## Optional Local (Non-Docker) Setup

### 1) Install dependencies

```bash
npm install
```

### 2) Configure environment

**Linux / macOS:**

```bash
cp backend/.env.example backend/.env
cp frontend/.env.example frontend/.env
```

**Windows (Command Prompt):**

```bash
copy backend\.env.example backend\.env
copy frontend\.env.example frontend\.env
```

Update values in `backend/.env` for your local MySQL instance.

### 3) Create database and run migrations

Create the database manually (example name: `studio_local`) and run:

```bash
npm run migrate
```

### 3.1) Run one-time bootstrap (required on clean DB)

Set bootstrap env values and run:

```bash
# Linux/macOS
BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_EMAIL=manager@local.test \
BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD='ChooseAStrongPassword!' \
npm run bootstrap --workspace backend
```

```powershell
# Windows PowerShell
$env:BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_EMAIL = "manager@local.test"
$env:BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD = "ChooseAStrongPassword!"
npm run bootstrap --workspace backend
```

The bootstrap script is idempotent and safe to re-run.

### 4) Start services (two terminals)

Terminal 1:

```bash
npm run dev:backend
```

Terminal 2:

```bash
npm run dev:frontend
```

### API base

- Backend base URL: `http://<local-ip>:4000/api/v1`
- Frontend dev URL: `http://<local-ip>:5173`

For LAN usage, keep backend `HOST=0.0.0.0` and Vite server host is already `0.0.0.0`.

## Role and access behavior

- Frontend role profile selector in the sidebar switches dashboard views and available routes.
- Backend enforces RBAC from authenticated Bearer sessions (`Authorization: Bearer <token>`).
- Protected endpoints require a valid session token and return `401` when missing/invalid/expired.
- Public endpoints are limited to `GET /api/v1/health`, `POST /api/v1/auth/register`, and `POST /api/v1/auth/login`.
- Dashboard metadata endpoint: `GET /api/v1/me/dashboard`.

## Resource and reservation policy rules

- Resources now store configurable policy fields:
  - `booking_window_days` (default `30`)
  - `min_duration_minutes` (default `30`)
  - `max_duration_minutes` (default `240`)
  - `early_check_in_minutes` (default `10`)
  - `late_check_in_grace_minutes` (default `15`)
  - `allow_slot_stitching` (default `true`)
- Reservation API enforces booking window, duration limits, 30-minute slot increments, and overlap prevention.
- Adjacent 30-minute reservations for the same member/resource are stitched into one continuous reservation when policy allows.
- Check-in endpoint: `POST /api/v1/reservations/:id/check-in`.
- No-show batch endpoint: `POST /api/v1/reservations/mark-no-shows`.

## Availability and conflict detection

- Availability browse endpoint: `GET /api/v1/availability` with query params:
  - `resource_id`
  - `user_id`
  - `date` (`YYYY-MM-DD`)
  - `duration_minutes` (30-minute increments)
  - optional `start_time` (`HH:mm`)
- Conflict explanations are returned in plain language for:
  - capacity exceeded
  - occupancy overlap
  - required 15-minute cleaning buffer
  - blocked resource
  - blacklist restriction
- Alternative slot suggestions are returned with each availability response.
- Additional policy entities:
  - `resource-blocks`
  - `reservation-blacklists`

## Account standing and booking restrictions

- Users have internal standing score (`0-100`) in `users.standing_score`.
- Standing is recalculated from `attendance_history` using configurable policy in
  `account_standing_policies`.
- Peak-hour restrictions apply when either condition is true:
  - standing score below threshold (default `< 60`)
  - no-shows in lookback window reach limit (default `>= 2` in 30 days)
- Restricted peak-hour bookings require approved manager override from
  `reservation_overrides`.
- API endpoints:
  - `GET /api/v1/account-standing-policy`
  - `PUT /api/v1/account-standing-policy/:id` (manager)
  - `GET /api/v1/account-standing/:userId`
  - `GET /api/v1/attendance-history?user_id=...`
  - `GET /api/v1/reservation-overrides`
  - `POST /api/v1/reservation-overrides` (manager)

## Calendar, holidays, and booking exceptions

- Holiday rules are stored in `holiday_rules` and editable at `/holiday-rules` in the UI.
- Calendar day rule endpoint: `GET /api/v1/calendar/day-rules?date=YYYY-MM-DD`.
- Availability view now surfaces holiday context and reduced-hour windows.
- Exception workflow for rule overrides:
  - Member/staff submits request with reason: `POST /api/v1/booking-exceptions`
  - Manager decides with required justification: `POST /api/v1/booking-exceptions/:id/decision`
  - List requests: `GET /api/v1/booking-exceptions`
- Reservation create/update accepts `exception_request_id`; approved matching exceptions can bypass standard rule conflicts.
- All exception requests and decisions are logged to `audit_trails` with reason text.

## Unified cart, pricing, coupons, and fulfillment split/merge

- Commerce catalog endpoint: `GET /api/v1/commerce/catalog`.
- Coupon list endpoint: `GET /api/v1/commerce/coupons`.
- Real-time cart quote endpoint: `POST /api/v1/commerce/cart/quote`.
  - Supports standard catalog `items` and `reservation_lines` in one payload.
- Checkout endpoint with split/merge mode: `POST /api/v1/commerce/checkout`.
  - `split_mode=auto_split` creates separate orders by fulfillment path.
  - `split_mode=merge_all` creates one merged order.
- Post-checkout operations:
  - `POST /api/v1/commerce/orders/:id/split`
  - `POST /api/v1/commerce/orders/merge`
- Coupon rules support fixed and percentage discounts with:
  - minimum subtotal threshold
  - category targeting (e.g., class packs)
  - max discount cap (e.g., 15% max $25)
- Seeded examples include:
  - `$10 off purchases over $75` (`SAVE10OVER75`)
  - `15% off class pack, max $25` (`CLASSPACK15`)

Availability-to-checkout flow:

- From `Availability Browser`, create a reservation from an available requested slot.
- This stores a checkout draft reservation line.
- Open `Unified Cart & Checkout` to see the reservation line merged with catalog items.

## Order state machine, idempotency, and local payments

- Orders now use explicit state transitions:
  - `pending_payment -> paid -> active|awaiting_pickup -> fulfilled`
  - `pending_payment -> cancelled|expired`
- Client idempotency keys are required for write operations (checkout/pay/cancel/transition/split/merge).
  - Provide via `x-idempotency-key` header or `idempotency_key` in request body.
- Unpaid orders auto-expire after 15 minutes using:
  - automatic background worker sweep (runs every minute by default)
  - optional manual trigger: `POST /api/v1/commerce/orders/expire-unpaid`
  - both paths release inventory/reservation holds and are idempotent.
- Supported local payment methods only:
  - `cash`
  - `card_terminal`
  - `gift_certificate`
- Manual payment references are stored only as masked text and `last_four`.
- Related persistence tables:
  - `idempotency_keys`
  - `order_state_events`
  - `order_holds`
  - `order_payments`

## Community feed and moderation

- Community feed endpoints:
  - `GET /api/v1/community/feed`
  - `POST /api/v1/community/posts` (supports replies via `parent_post_id`)
  - `POST /api/v1/community/posts/:id/report`
  - `GET /api/v1/community/reports/mine`
- Moderation endpoints:
  - `GET /api/v1/community/moderation/queue`
  - `POST /api/v1/community/moderation/posts/:id/decision`
  - `POST /api/v1/community/moderation/reports/:id/decision`
- Anti-abuse controls:
  - account throttle: max posts/hour (default 10)
  - optional IP/device throttle limits
  - local CAPTCHA challenges via `POST /api/v1/community/captcha/challenge`
  - allowlist/blocklist rules (`community-rules` table)
  - temporary/permanent bans (`community-bans` table)
- Report statuses are visible to reporters via `community/reports/mine`.

## Security, audit, and reconciliation

- Authentication now supports salted password hashing (`scrypt`) via:
  - `POST /api/v1/auth/register`
  - `POST /api/v1/auth/login`
- Repeated login failures trigger lockout:
  - account locked for 15 minutes after 8 failed attempts
  - events written to queryable `security_events`
- Sensitive APIs support optional anti-replay request timestamps using
  `x-request-timestamp` within configured window.
- Permission changes are tracked with complete audit trail:
  - only manager role with `security.permissions.manage` may update permissions
  - self-grant of privileged permissions is explicitly denied
  - `PUT /api/v1/security/users/:id/permissions`
- Fine issuance and refund processing are auditable and financial-log backed:
  - `POST /api/v1/fines`
  - `POST /api/v1/refunds/:id/process`
- Financial logs are tamper-evident with chained hashes in `financial_logs`.
- Reconciliation APIs:
  - `GET /api/v1/reconciliation/daily?date=YYYY-MM-DD`
  - `POST /api/v1/reconciliation/shift-close`
  - `GET /api/v1/reconciliation/shift/:shiftKey`
- Variance flag is raised when drawer counted total differs from expected by more than `$5.00`.
